chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Customer
triplets.object: an individual or entity registered on the platform

triplets.subject: Sales Order
triplets.object: agreement between platform and Customer to supply Products

triplets.subject: Sales Order
triplets.predicate: has one or more Order Line Items

triplets.subject: Order Line Item
triplets.predicate: part of exactly one Sales Order

triplets.subject: Product
triplets.predicate: appears on zero or more Order Line Items

triplets.subject: Payment
triplets.predicate: is for exactly one Sales Order

triplets.subject: Return Policy
triplets.object: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund

triplets.subject: ORDER_LINE_ITEM
triplets.object: Individual product lines within a sales order

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: An Order Line Item is the individual product line on a Sales Order, representing one product reference and its quantity within that order.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Must contain at least one Order Line Item | Key Attributes: - References exactly one Product | Key Attributes: - Quantity of units ordered | Key Attributes: - Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Order Line Item Relationships**:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order | Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order. | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | SALES_ORDER

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Payment is a payment transaction associated with a Sales Order, identified by PAYMENT_ID and linked to the order via PAYMENT.ORDER_ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Payment Relationships**:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Payment          → PAYMENT | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID | **Key Relationships Summary**
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: The Shipment entity represents the logistics delivery of items from a warehouse to a customer, tied to a single sales order and containing shipping details such as shipped timestamp, tracking number, source warehouse, destination address, and status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Shipment**
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: The physical delivery of Products from a Warehouse to a Customer's delivery address.

**Business Context**: Shipments represent the logistics side of order fulfillment. They track what items were sent, when, and to whom.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique Shipment ID
- References exactly one Sales Order
- Source Warehouse
- Destination Customer Address
- Shipped timestamp
- Tracking number
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Column Details**:
| SHIPPED_AT | DATETIME | Ship timestamp | YES | | **Column Details**:
| TRACKING_NUMBER | VARCHAR(100) | Carrier tracking number | YES | | **Co

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master(Cust Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A database table containing customer master data, identified by the CUST_ID key, and referenced as a foreign key by other tables (e.g., SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | **Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID) | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A shipping address associated with a customer used as the destination for shipments in the e-commerce system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Definition: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | Business Context: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | Key Attributes: - One or more delivery/billing addresses | Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment) | | CUST_ID | INT | Unique customer identifier | NO | | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

entities_post_resolution.name: Return Policy
entities_post_resolution.definition: Refund is a reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund. | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

entities_post_resolution.name: Exactly One Customer
entities_post_resolution.definition: exactly one Customer: a data governance constraint mandating that a reference or relationship points to exactly one Customer entity.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Key Attributes: - References exactly one Customer | Key Attributes: - References exactly one Product

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Price
entities_post_resolution.definition: Unit price is the price charged for one unit of a product or service, expressed in the base currency and potentially tied to a specific time (e.g., at order).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Key Attributes: - Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | Key Attributes: - Unit price in base currency (EUR)

entities_post_resolution.name: 7 Years After
entities_post_resolution.definition: Data retained for 7 years after last purchase
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Data retained for 7 years after last purchase

entities_post_resolution.name: Active/Inactive
entities_post_resolution.definition: Key Attributes: - Active/inactive status
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Key Attributes: - Active/inactive status

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core customer records including identifying information, contact details, regional assignment, account creation timestamp, and activation status.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, naming, categorization, pricing, and availability status.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores hierarchical categories for products, including each category's name and a reference to its parent category.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores the header information for sales orders, including customer reference, order date, total amount, status, and timestamps for payment confirmation, delivery, and record creation.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores the line items for each order, including the product, quantity, unit price, and total amount for the line.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of payments processed for orders, including the method, amount, status, and timestamps for when the payment was confirmed and created.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments linked to customer orders, including destination warehouse, shipment timestamp, tracking details, and current shipment status.
